name: Chicken
careNotes: Needs daily feeding when it cannot eat grass outside.
Daily petting and good mood help product quality.
beginnerNote: Chickens are the simplest first animal path and teach the basic feed, pet, collect loop.

name: Duck
unlock: Available after upgrading to a Big Coop.
careNotes: Needs the same daily care loop as other coop animals.
Duck Feather production is less routine than egg production.
beginnerNote: Ducks are useful after a Big Coop when you want more product variety and bundle coverage.

name: Rabbit
unlock: Available after upgrading to a Deluxe Coop.
careNotes: Needs daily feeding and care.
Rabbit's Foot is an occasional product, so do not plan around it as a guaranteed daily item.
beginnerNote: Rabbits are a later coop animal for wool and rare product coverage rather than an immediate first purchase.

name: Dinosaur
unlock: Hatched from a Dinosaur Egg in an upgraded coop incubator.
careNotes: Requires an incubator path rather than direct purchase.
Production timing is slower than regular chickens.
beginnerNote: Dinosaurs are a special coop path; treat them as a discovery or collection animal, not a first-animal goal.

name: Void Chicken
unlock: Hatched from a Void Egg in an upgraded coop incubator.
careNotes: Uses the coop animal care loop.
Requires access to a Void Egg before it can be hatched.
beginnerNote: Void Chickens are a special coop option for Void Eggs once your save has the setup for them.

name: Golden Chicken
unlock: Late-game unlock after reaching perfection-related progression.
careNotes: Late-game animal; not relevant to normal Year 1 planning.
Uses the coop animal care loop after hatching.
beginnerNote: Golden Chickens are a late-game goal and should not affect early coop planning.

name: Cow
careNotes: Needs daily feeding when it cannot eat grass outside.
Requires milking to collect milk.
beginnerNote: Cows are the simplest barn animal and introduce the milk and dairy production path.

name: Goat
unlock: Available after upgrading to a Big Barn.
careNotes: Needs daily care and feeding.
Requires milking to collect goat milk.
beginnerNote: Goats expand the barn product path after you have upgraded beyond the basic Barn.

name: Sheep
unlock: Available after upgrading to a Deluxe Barn.
careNotes: Needs daily care and feeding.
Requires shearing to collect wool.
beginnerNote: Sheep are a later barn animal for wool access and are not usually the first reason to build a Barn.

name: Pig
unlock: Available after upgrading to a Deluxe Barn.
careNotes: Needs outdoor access to find truffles.
Does not find truffles in winter.
beginnerNote: Pigs are powerful but expensive late-barn animals; plan feed, space, and season timing before buying them.

name: Ostrich
unlock: Hatched from an Ostrich Egg using the Ostrich Incubator.
careNotes: Special barn animal unlocked through later progression.
Not part of normal first-year animal planning.
beginnerNote: Ostriches are a later-game barn path and should be treated as a special progression animal.
